name: Sandra Bernhard
blurb: The theatrical solo-show pioneer — stand-up that fused cabaret, rock-and-roll, and Vanity Fair celebrity dissection a decade before anyone called it 'a show.'
bio: Sandra Bernhard wrote the template for the modern one-woman show in the late '80s and almost no one has matched it since. Without You I'm Nothing — staged off-Broadway in 1988, filmed in 1990 — is the canonical text: a 90-minute set that moves between stand-up, lounge singing, monologue, Diana Ross drag, and astonishing real songs, all of it staking out a space between Lenny Bruce, Bette Midler at the Continental Baths, and the celebrity-culture autopsy she'd been doing on Letterman since 1983. She announced herself in The King of Comedy (1981, as Masha — National Society of Film Critics Award for Best Supporting Actress), held a six-year recurring role on Roseanne as Nancy Bartlett (one of TV's first openly bisexual recurring characters), and has hosted SiriusXM's Sandyland on Radio Andy daily for a decade running. She is on Comedy Central's 100 Greatest Stand-Ups of All Time and stands as a foundational figure for theatrical comedy.

timeline.ev: Moves to Los Angeles; starts doing stand-up at The Comedy Store
The King of Comedy (Scorsese) — plays Masha; wins National Society of Film Critics Award for Best Supporting Actress
First Late Night with David Letterman appearance (21 February); will go on to make nearly 30 appearances on Letterman's shows
Confessions of a Pretty Lady — first book, a memoir/manifesto
Without You I'm Nothing — six-month off-Broadway run at the Orpheum Theatre; surprise Letterman appearance with Madonna becomes a TV-comedy landmark
Without You I'm Nothing — film version released (dir. John Boskovich, exec-prod Nicolas Roeg)
Joins Roseanne as Nancy Bartlett; plays the role through 1997
I'm Still Here…Damn It! — Broadway-bound solo show (HBO special)
Plan B from Outer Space tour
Begins hosting Sandyland on SiriusXM Radio Andy
Cast as Nurse Judy Kubrak on FX's Pose (recurring through 2021)
Cast as Nurse Cecily on Apple TV+'s Severance

accolades: National Society of Film Critics Award for Best Supporting Actress (The King of Comedy, 1983)
Comedy Central's 100 Greatest Stand-Up Comedians of All Time
Gracie Award for Sandyland (SiriusXM Radio Andy)
Author of four books, including Confessions of a Pretty Lady and Love, Love, and Love
Recurring role on Roseanne (1991–97) — one of network television's first openly bisexual recurring characters

appearances.plat: Live tour — debuted at Joe's Pub NYC, no official video
Live tour — no official video release
MCMI/New Line (film) — released theatrically; later home video

appearances.t: I Love Being Me, Don't You?

specials.plat: Live tour — debuted at Joe's Pub NYC, no official video
Live tour — no official video release
MCMI/New Line (film) — released theatrically; later home video
